title: Farmers Market

jokes.text: And I have to say that the way that you're rockin' those slacks is quite impressive as well.
jokes.setup: Professional meeting setting where Leslie should maintain professional boundaries
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately compliments Ben's appearance in an overly familiar way
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie character moment - her inability to maintain professional boundaries even when she should

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with acknowledging the fantastic work of one of your employees, especially if she's adorable.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to justify the inappropriate workplace flirting
jokes.punchline: Calling your employee 'adorable' in a professional setting
jokes.explanation: Ben's awkward attempt to rationalize inappropriate workplace behavior

jokes.text: And we finish each other's... Sentences! We rehearsed that at home. Naked in bed.
jokes.setup: Cute couple finishing each other's sentences
jokes.punchline: The overshare about rehearsing naked in bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from cute to uncomfortable overshare, very character-specific

jokes.text: What! What!
jokes.setup: Their immediate realization they overshared
jokes.punchline: Synchronized horrified reactions
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat, economical delivery

jokes.text: I think I'm in love with you. Oh, my God, that's great news, 'cause I'm in love with you too.
jokes.setup: Professional meeting continuing to devolve
jokes.punchline: Full romantic declarations during a work meeting
jokes.explanation: Escalates the workplace inappropriateness to absurd levels

jokes.text: Stamp the damn form.
jokes.setup: Romantic chaos happening around him
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical, no-nonsense response cutting through the nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - cutting through BS with brutal efficiency

jokes.text: Group hug! Little G.H. Bring it in.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to include Ron in their love fest
jokes.punchline: Abbreviating 'group hug' and forcing affection on Ron
jokes.explanation: Leslie's oblivious enthusiasm contrasting with Ron's clear discomfort

jokes.text: I think our love made him angry. I think he broke my wrist. I'm not kidding. Really?
jokes.setup: Group hug attempt with Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron apparently injuring them in the hug
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that perfectly captures Ron's violent aversion to affection

jokes.text: Whales don't have feet.
jokes.setup: Ann complaining about being a whale with foot pain
jokes.punchline: Chris's literal-minded correction
jokes.explanation: Chris's characteristic literal interpretation missing the emotional point

jokes.text: This morning when I got out of bed, I just tipped forward like a poorly made bowling pin.
jokes.setup: Ann describing her pregnancy clumsiness
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to a poorly made bowling pin
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific imagery that makes the physical comedy relatable and visual

jokes.text: I read something on raddadsolutions.net that there's a pressure point in the foot that could induce labor.
jokes.setup: Chris offering to rub Ann's feet
jokes.punchline: Overly specific website reference and pregnancy paranoia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - over-researched dad anxiety

jokes.text: Everything is amazing. Today is perfect, and I love you. He's driving me up the fucking wall.
jokes.setup: Ann's talking head seeming positive about Chris
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to honest frustration
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - builds false expectation then subverts completely

jokes.text: Look at this tiny tree. Can you eat this? Aww. This one's dead.
jokes.setup: Leslie exploring the farmers market
jokes.punchline: Her complete ignorance about plants and nature
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's disconnect from nature despite creating a farmers market

jokes.text: That's the fun thing about cabbage. One of the fun things.
jokes.setup: Vendor trying to sell cabbage enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Implying there are multiple fun things about cabbage
jokes.explanation: Deadpan delivery of absurd enthusiasm for mundane vegetable

jokes.text: 'Chardbodies: Get a chard on'? Let's get 're-charded' in here!
jokes.setup: Sexualized vegetable stand name
jokes.punchline: Terrible chard puns being shouted enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Groan-worthy puns but the absurdity of sexualizing chard makes it work

jokes.text: Let's get 're-charded' in here!
jokes.setup: Already established chard sexual puns
jokes.punchline: 'Retarded' pun with chard
jokes.explanation: Weak continuation of pun theme, relies on offensive word substitution

jokes.text: Mmm, chard is so bitter.
jokes.setup: Sexy chard marketing
jokes.punchline: Customer's honest reaction to actually tasting it
jokes.explanation: Reality check moment that undercuts the sexy marketing with truth

jokes.text: By 'rules of conduct,' do you mean that wooden sign you made that says, 'Peas be kind to others'?
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming vendors are violating rules
jokes.punchline: Revealing her 'rules' are just vegetable puns on a wooden sign
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character beat - making pun-based rules for serious situations

jokes.text: Yes, and 'In case of fire, romaine calm,' and 'You won't bay leaf how nice olive our vendors are.'
jokes.setup: Ben's mockery of her vegetable pun rules
jokes.punchline: Leslie doubling down with even more elaborate vegetable puns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cascade of vegetable puns that's quintessentially Leslie - elaborate wordplay for bureaucracy

jokes.text: I don't like vegetables, but I'm very good at vegetable puns.
jokes.setup: Leslie's elaborate vegetable pun rules
jokes.punchline: She admits she doesn't even like vegetables
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - her skill doesn't match her interest

jokes.text: Cabbage! Come and... get it. Connie, no. Put those away.
jokes.setup: Connie trying to compete with the sexy chard stand
jokes.punchline: Implied inappropriate display by the cabbage vendor
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy showing how the chard stand is corrupting other vendors

jokes.text: No, that's a super small piano.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Andy plays guitar
jokes.punchline: April's sarcastic correction about the obviously small guitar
jokes.explanation: Classic April deadpan sarcasm, perfectly delivered

jokes.setup: Negotiating payment for playing a kids' party
jokes.punchline: Andy's complete misunderstanding of money amounts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - completely disconnected from reality about money

jokes.text: 150 bucks? Split that four ways, boom. $600.
jokes.setup: Andy calculating the band's split
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong math that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Hilariously wrong math that's perfectly in character for Andy

jokes.text: I can't wash my face with hand soap. Look at these pores. They're gaping.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about workplace amenities
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of his pores
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom vanity and self-obsession over minor details

jokes.text: It's the whine & cheese club, gorgeous.
jokes.setup: Ann asking what they're doing
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'wine and cheese' that explains their complaining session
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly describes what they're doing

jokes.text: 'cause I have a beach ball in my stomach that's punching on my bladder.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining why she has to pee frequently
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of pregnancy as a violent beach ball
jokes.explanation: Creative imagery that makes pregnancy discomfort both relatable and funny

jokes.text: I can't have cheese, Larry! And I can't have wine either.
jokes.setup: Larry offering cheese to pregnant Ann
jokes.punchline: Ann's frustrated outburst about pregnancy restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's pregnancy frustration building

jokes.text: I want to be Pac-Man, and instead of dots, I want 'em to be cinnamon buns.
jokes.setup: Ann listing foods she craves
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about being Pac-Man eating cinnamon buns
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd pregnancy craving fantasy that's both specific and relatable

jokes.text: I want to be a giant head and a mouth, and I just want to eat rows and rows of junk food pellets
jokes.setup: Continuing the Pac-Man fantasy
jokes.punchline: Even more elaborate description of wanting to just be a eating machine
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd fantasy to logical extreme

jokes.text: The reason is it's vegetable porn. Porn on the cob.
jokes.setup: Ben asking for a reason to kick out the chard vendors
jokes.punchline: 'Porn on the cob' - corn/vegetable pun
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that combines the situation with Leslie's love of wordplay

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I'm just very good at that.
jokes.setup: Leslie making another vegetable pun
jokes.punchline: Her proud acknowledgment of her punning ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Leslie's pride in her ridiculous skill

jokes.text: Like we only talk about work stuff when we're at city hall, like a firewall system.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting they separate work and home life
jokes.punchline: Using tech terminology 'firewall' for relationship boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's nerdy approach to problems

jokes.text: No, it's ridiculous to think that you could survive a nuclear blast by jumping into a refrigerator.
jokes.setup: They're watching Indiana Jones Crystal Skull
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate criticism of the movie's logic
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's tendency to overthink even entertainment

jokes.text: Firewall, man. We pulled into the parking lot, and this is technically city hall property, so now we're at work-- let's do this.
jokes.setup: Ben's firewall concept
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately exploiting a technicality to break the firewall
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - finding bureaucratic loopholes to get what she wants

jokes.text: The surprise is there's toffee in the inside.
jokes.setup: Leslie offering 'toffee surprise' treats
jokes.punchline: The completely obvious 'surprise'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - making something special out of the mundane

jokes.text: My stomach has so many stretch marks on it that it looks like an old-fashioned globe.
jokes.setup: Ann describing pregnancy symptoms
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to an old-fashioned globe
jokes.explanation: Vivid, relatable imagery that makes pregnancy symptoms both funny and touching

jokes.text: I've been reading up on nipples. It has nipple cream, nipple pads, and also a special nipple pimple ointment in case you develop any pimples on your nipples.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to help with pregnancy symptoms
jokes.punchline: Saying 'nipple' repeatedly while being overly helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - over-helpful to the point of discomfort

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you have to stop using the word 'nipple.'
jokes.setup: Chris's repeated use of 'nipple'
jokes.punchline: Ann's desperate plea to stop
jokes.explanation: Natural reaction to Chris's awkward oversharing

jokes.text: boob hats.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to avoid saying 'nipples'
jokes.punchline: His awkward alternative term 'boob hats'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, absurd alternative that's somehow more awkward than the original word

jokes.text: ♪ Super fun ♪ It feels super good ♪ I wanna do it all the time ♪ Sex is cool
jokes.setup: Band practicing for a kids' party
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate lyrics for children
jokes.explanation: Shows the band's complete inappropriateness for children's entertainment

jokes.text: Did you book us a gig at a kids' party? You said we were playing at a festival. You said Dave Grohl might be there.
jokes.setup: Discovering they're playing for kids
jokes.punchline: The absurd lie about Dave Grohl being at a 6-year-old's party
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Andy misled his bandmates

jokes.text: Dave Grohl might be there. I don't know. He might be anywhere. The guy's awesome, and he's unpredictable.
jokes.setup: Confronted about lying about Dave Grohl
jokes.punchline: Andy's illogical defense that Grohl could be anywhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - completely absurd but delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: I'm sick of all the drama with this band. I-I quit. I'm going back to rabbinical school. Umbilical school?
jokes.setup: Band member quitting dramatically
jokes.punchline: Andy mishearing 'rabbinical' as 'umbilical'
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy mishearing that makes no sense but is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's like, 'Hey, you like lettuce? Try this. It's worse. It tastes like kale took a dump on spinach.'
jokes.setup: Nolan explaining why chard needs sexy marketing
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate description of chard's taste
jokes.explanation: Hilariously accurate food description that explains the character's motivation

jokes.text: I like to think of it as celery with B.O.
jokes.setup: Nolan's description of chard
jokes.punchline: Leslie's own gross but funny description
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective gross-out description that shows Leslie can be funny too

jokes.text: What? There's no such thing. Yes, there is. I made it for you last night.
jokes.setup: Ben dismissing Leslie's mention of an approval pen
jokes.punchline: Leslie revealing she literally made him a custom pen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - creating elaborate solutions for simple problems

jokes.text: Yeah! It's sushi and cigar club now.
jokes.setup: The group kicking Ann out of their complaining club
jokes.punchline: They've upgraded to more luxurious vices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how they've moved on from their original concept

jokes.text: And you know what? I would yell some more, but I think I just peed my pants a little bit.
jokes.setup: Ann's angry rant about being excluded
jokes.punchline: Pregnancy reality interrupting her dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with pregnancy reality

jokes.text: 'Dear honey--' Oh, how sweet. Read.
jokes.setup: Larry reading Ben's note to Leslie
jokes.punchline: Larry getting distracted by the romantic greeting
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry moment - getting sidetracked by irrelevant details

jokes.text: All roads and bridges fall under the purview of the Pawnee department of transportation, which is located on the fourth floor of city hall. Firewall down.
jokes.setup: Ben thinking he's safe on the road
jokes.punchline: Leslie's bureaucratic knowledge defeating his escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - using bureaucratic expertise as a superpower

jokes.text: Bank! Private property. Firewall restored! Ha-ha-ha!
jokes.setup: The firewall chase continuing
jokes.punchline: Ben's triumphant declaration of safety
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the absurd chase with Ben's temporary victory

jokes.text: You can't escape city hall, fool! Let's dance.
jokes.setup: Leslie revealing the water source defeats Ben's fountain sanctuary
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic challenge to continue the battle
jokes.explanation: Great escalation to action movie territory with Leslie as unstoppable force

jokes.text: Tom put all my records into this rectangle!
jokes.setup: Ron discovering digital music
jokes.punchline: His confused description of an iPod as a 'rectangle'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - his complete bafflement by technology

jokes.text: The songs just play one right after the other! This is an excellent rectangle!
jokes.setup: Ron's confusion about the iPod
jokes.punchline: His amazement at basic playlist functionality
jokes.explanation: Continues the perfect Ron technology confusion with childlike wonder

jokes.text: I feel like I'm arguing with the sun.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining why he doesn't argue with Leslie
jokes.punchline: Comparing Leslie to an unstoppable force of nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures Leslie's overwhelming enthusiasm

jokes.text: What? That is totally crazy! I am super chill all the time!
jokes.setup: Ben saying Leslie is too passionate
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate denial that she's passionate
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-contradiction that exemplifies Leslie's complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: This is 'un-charded' territory for us.
jokes.setup: Ben discussing their unusual work situation
jokes.punchline: Terrible chard pun in serious moment
jokes.explanation: Ben attempting Leslie's punning style with mixed results

jokes.text: I can't because my legs are numb. Yeah, I can't move. Are we gonna die here?
jokes.setup: Standing in freezing fountain water
jokes.punchline: Their dramatic realization they're stuck
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy consequence of their ridiculous standoff

jokes.text: Please welcome Johnny Karate and his magical guitar stick!
jokes.setup: April introducing Andy at the kids' party
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous stage name and description improvised on the spot
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Andy's transformation into children's performer

jokes.text: ♪ Pick your nose ♪ 'Cause boogers are gross ♪ Clean out your snout
jokes.setup: Andy improvising kid-appropriate songs
jokes.punchline: Hilariously gross but somehow perfect kids' song
jokes.explanation: Andy's natural ability to connect with kids through gross-out humor

jokes.text: ♪ Everybody pees the bed ♪ Just something that we do ♪ I for instance peed the bed ♪ Until I was 32
jokes.setup: Andy's kid-friendly song about universal experiences
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his own extended bedwetting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy overshare that's somehow both inappropriate and endearing

jokes.text: This is so fun. I cannot believe this only cost me 150 bucks.
jokes.setup: Watching Andy's chaotic children's performance
jokes.punchline: April's genuine enjoyment at the bargain price
jokes.explanation: April's rare moment of genuine satisfaction with Andy's success

jokes.text: There's just been, like, a long, slow fart stream coming out of me since we started talking... this morning.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining her gassiness to Chris
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of continuous flatulence
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but real pregnancy symptom delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: That really sucks. Yeah. It does. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Chris applying the advice about just listening
jokes.punchline: The simple response actually working perfectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the relationship advice subplot

jokes.text: This town is full of lunatics.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the nighttime sexy vegetable market
jokes.punchline: Ben's resigned acceptance of Pawnee's weirdness
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben reaction to Pawnee's absurdity

jokes.text: Oh, my God, this was a terrible idea. Yeah, it's like a tree barfed in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben trying chard
jokes.punchline: Their disgusted reactions confirming how awful it tastes
jokes.explanation: Good ending tag that confirms everyone's opinion about chard

tmdb_overview: On Ann and Chris's last night in Pawnee, Leslie throws a grand farewell party and attempts to fulfill one last promise. Meanwhile, Tom, Ben, Ron and Andy struggle to find the perfect going away gift for Chris.
insight: Observational humor drives a modest 78.5-score hour built on character consistency over big laughs.
